items.title: MAINT: Modernize Catboost tutorial notebook to use shap.plots API
items.body:
## Description

This PR modernizes the `Catboost tutorial.ipynb` notebook to ensure it is fully compliant with the repository's current maintenance standards and the new `Explanation` object API. 

It specifically addresses the remaining legacy plotting functions in the tree-based model examples.

**Key changes:**
- Replaced the legacy `shap.dependence_plot` call with the modern equivalent: `shap.plots.scatter`.
- Verified the notebook execution to ensure all cells run cleanly and visualizations render correctly with the modern backend.

This update contributes to the ongoing effort to keep the documentation current and runnable for new users.

**Relates to Meta Issue #3036** ([Meta-issue] Notebooks are outdated / non-runnable)

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature) *(N/A: Documentation/Notebook update)*

items.title: MAINT: Modernize LightGBM Census notebook to use shap.Explanation API
items.body:
## Overview
Closes #3036

This PR modernizes the `Census income classification with LightGBM.ipynb` notebook to use the standard, unified `shap.Explanation` object and `shap.plots` API, aligning it with current library best practices.

**Key changes:**
- Replaced legacy `shap.force_plot` and `shap.summary_plot` calls to their modern equivalents: `shap.plots.force` and `shap.plots.beeswarm`.
- Migrated legacy `shap.dependence_plot` calls to `shap.plots.scatter`.
- Configured `shap_values.display_data = X_display.values` to ensure plots correctly display human-readable categorical labels rather than encoded integers.
- Re-executed the notebook to ensure all cells run cleanly and all visualizations render beautifully with the modern backend.

This ensures consistency across the tabular tree-based model examples (following the recent XGBoost update).

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature) *(N/A: Documentation/Notebook update)*

items.title: MAINT: Modernize XGBoost Census notebook to use shap.Explanation API
items.body:
## Overview
Closes #3036

This PR modernizes the `Census income classification with XGBoost.ipynb` notebook to use the standard, unified `shap.Explanation` object and `shap.plots` API, aligning it with current library best practices.

**Key changes:**
- Replaced legacy `shap.TreeExplainer(model).shap_values(X)` with `shap.TreeExplainer(model)(X)` to correctly generate `Explanation` objects.
- Migrated legacy `shap.force_plot` and `shap.summary_plot` calls to their modern equivalents: `shap.plots.force`, `shap.plots.beeswarm`, and `shap.plots.bar`.
- Migrated legacy `shap.dependence_plot` calls to `shap.plots.scatter`.
- Configured `shap_values.display_data = X_display.values` to ensure plots correctly display human-readable categorical labels rather than encoded integers.
- Re-executed the notebook to ensure all cells run cleanly and all visualizations render beautifully with the modern backend.

Updating this classic notebook ensures new users reading the documentation learn the correct modern API syntax right away, without encountering legacy patterns.

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature) *(N/A: Documentation/Notebook update)*

items.title: MAINT: Silence NumPy global RNG deprecation warnings in test suite
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3066

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:

As part of the ongoing "janitor work" tracked in #3066 to address deprecation warnings in the test suite, this PR silences the frequent `FutureWarning` spam regarding NumPy's global random state (`np.random.seed`).

**Context:**
Functions like `shap.plots.beeswarm` intentionally warn users when the global RNG is seeded, encouraging the use of an explicit `rng` object instead. However, the legacy SHAP test suite intentionally relies on `np.random.seed(0)` to ensure perfectly deterministic image generation for `pytest-mpl` visual comparisons.

Attempting to modernize these tests by passing an explicit `rng` object (e.g., `rs = np.random.RandomState(0)`) breaks the visual baselines. This occurs because SHAP internally casts the `RandomState` object using `np.random.default_rng(rng)`, forcefully upgrading the random generator from the legacy `MT19937` algorithm to the modern `PCG64` algorithm, which diverges the pseudo-random streams.

**Solution:**
To preserve visual test reproducibility while drastically cleaning up the `pytest` output logs, this PR applies a targeted module-level `pytestmark = pytest.mark.filterwarnings` to explicitly ignore this intentional warning inside the affected test modules. 

Additionally, this PR fixes a missing `show=False` parameter in `test_summary_plot_interaction` that was causing a `UserWarning: FigureCanvasAgg is non-interactive` when the test suite was run under `-W error`.

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

items.title: DOCS: Modernize Census income XGBoost notebook to use Explanation object API
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3036

This PR modernizes the heavily-used `Census income classification with XGBoost.ipynb` tutorial to eliminate legacy deprecation warnings and showcase the modern `Explanation` object plotting APIs.

Changes made:
- Upgraded the explainer extraction to use the modern `explainer(X)` callable.
- Leveraged `shap_values.display_data` to globally map categorical strings instead of raw numbers.
- Replaced all legacy `shap.force_plot` calls with `shap.plots.force`.
- Replaced all legacy `shap.summary_plot` calls with native `shap.plots.bar` and `shap.plots.beeswarm`.
- Replaced all legacy `shap.dependence_plot` calls with the new `shap.plots.scatter`.

## Checklist

- [x] All pre-commit checks pass.

items.title: Refactor: Add native Explanation object support to decision_plot and modernize notebook
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3036
Closes #3411

This PR tackles two modernization meta-issues simultaneously by upgrading the `decision_plot` API and completely rewriting its corresponding tutorial notebook.

1. **API Consistency (#3411)**: `shap.plots.decision` previously did not natively support the modern `Explanation` object, forcing users to manually extract base values and arrays. I have updated the `decision` signature to natively intercept `Explanation` objects when passed as the first argument, bringing it inline with the modern `waterfall` and `violin` APIs.
2. **Notebook Modernization (#3036)**: With the new API support, I completely rewrote the `decision_plot.ipynb` tutorial to replace all legacy `explainer.shap_values()` calls with the modern `explainer(X)` callable, and updated all 15+ plotting cells to pass the `Explanation` object directly to `shap.plots.decision()`.

All backwards compatibility is maintained, and all internal `decision_plot` tests pass successfully locally.

## Checklist

- [x] All pre-commit checks pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (existing tests pass flawlessly).

items.title: Refactor: Implement explicit ax parameter support for decision plots
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3411

This PR modernizes the `shap.plots.decision` function (and `decision_plot` alias) to support an explicit `ax` parameter, as part of the broader effort to improve the plotting API consistency (addressing Meta-issue #3411). By transitioning from stateful `matplotlib.pyplot` calls to an object-oriented `Axes` API within `__decision_plot_matplotlib`, users can now seamlessly integrate decision plots into custom subplots. All internal tests pass successfully.

## Checklist

- [x] All pre-commit checks pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (existing tests pass flawlessly).

items.title: DOCS: Update violin notebook with modern API and improved explanations
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3036

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:
I am opening this PR to take over the work from #4741 and update the `violin` notebook. 

Here is what I changed:
- Set `random_state` for the XGBoost model to guarantee perfect reproducibility.
- Migrated all code to the modern `shap.Explainer` API.
- Removed redundant `features` and `feature_names` parameters from the plotting calls since the modern `Explanation` object includes them natively.
- Updated the markdown explanations to reflect the modern API usage.
- Ran the notebook end-to-end to verify everything executes flawlessly.

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [ ] Unit tests added (not applicable for notebook updates)

items.title: Refactor: Implement explicit ax parameter support for waterfall plots
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3411 

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:
As part of the initiative to improve plotting API consistency across the library, this PR refactors both the `waterfall` and `waterfall_legacy` functions. The functions now accept an explicit `ax` parameter, and all global `plt.*` plotting calls have been migrated to their equivalent `ax.*` methods. The functions have also been updated to return the `ax` object, ensuring standard API behavior.

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [ ] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

items.title: Fix: Honor custom ax parameter in partial_dependence_plot
items.body:
## Overview

Closes #3206 

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:
This PR resolves an issue where `partial_dependence_plot` implicitly relies on the global `plt.gcf()` and `plt.gca()` state, causing overlapping subplots when iterating over multiple features. The logic has been updated to explicitly resolve and respect the user-provided `ax` parameter by utilizing `ax.get_figure()`.

## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [ ] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)
